affordances.name: Reuse proven methods with present-fit checks
affordances.mechanism: When a tested method or protocol can prevent reinvention, use time-tested validation as a starting point while checking whether its assumptions, reference class, and original validation still fit current conditions.

use_when: The team needs an already-tested method, protocol, or evaluative lens to avoid reinventing a solved discipline from scratch.
Prior validation provides a trustworthy starting point that can accelerate diagnosis, comparison, or execution.
Speed and reliability matter more than novelty, but the framework still needs to be checked against current conditions.
do_not_use_when: Time-tested gets treated as self-justifying authority.
Nobody rechecks whether the original validation still matches the present environment.
Competitive adaptation or changed conditions make historical validation obsolete.
case_evidence_needed: The proven method, protocol, framework, or reference class being reused.
The assumptions under the original validation.
The current case conditions that match or violate those assumptions.
The minimum evidence needed to prove or disprove fit.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to line up the assumptions behind a proven method and test current-case fit before recommending reuse.
evidence_required: The time-tested method or reference class.
The assumptions under its historical success.
The present conditions that may differ.
The evidence needed to confirm fit or trigger first-principles rework.
good_output_shape: Warns against authority-by-tradition when conditions changed.

diagnostic_questions: Which tested method or reference class applies, and what assumptions made it valid?
Do current conditions still match the original validation?
Are we using time-tested structure to reduce risk, or to avoid rethinking changed conditions?
misuse_guards: Do not treat time-tested as proof by itself.
Do not reuse a framework without checking present fit.
Do not let validation become confirmation of a desired hypothesis.

source_evidence.source_quote: already-tested method, protocol, or evaluative lens
line up all the assumptions that underpin a reference class
original validation still matches the present environment

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 17 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports proven methods as starting points that require reference-class and present-fit validation.
dropped_material: Did not treat historical precedent as sufficient proof.
Did not extract a preference for tradition over first-principles re-derivation.
open_questions: Later packet review should inspect overlap with tradition-vs-innovation-balance and first-principles-thinking.

absence_records.reason: The source warns against treating time-tested status as self-justifying authority; it does not support historical validation as proof by itself.
The source requires checking current conditions; it does not support deferring to tradition when original validation no longer fits.

source_evidence.source_quote: framework still needs to be checked against current conditions

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 17 treats time-tested validation as disciplined reuse with assumption and fit checks.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, tradition selector, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: A future packet should test whether this card reduces reinvention without creating authority bias.
